shloka:
श्री भगवानुवाच
परं भूयः प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम्।
यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनयः सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गताः।।14.1।।
translation: The Supreme Lord said: I shall again reveal to you the supreme wisdom, the best of all knowledge, knowing which all the sages have attained the highest perfection.
purport:
Just as Arjuna was overwhelmed by the chaos of the battlefield and the deep confusion in his own mind, modern individuals often feel lost in the complexity of life, unsure of why they or others behave in certain ways. When life feels unpredictable, we naturally look for a framework to make sense of the world.

In this verse, Krishna sets the stage by promising to reveal the ultimate framework—the supreme knowledge. He is preparing to explain the hidden psychological forces that drive all human behavior. The core principle here is that true freedom from stress and suffering begins with deep understanding, not just blind action.

Before you can fix a personal problem or improve your mindset, you must thoroughly understand the system operating behind the scenes. Recognizing that there is a clear, objective knowledge capable of cutting through our daily confusion is the essential first step toward finding lasting peace and focus.
